mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling window, months with positive PAYEMS change coincide with positive 20-day SPX returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above 2.5% tightens financial conditions, raises corporate borrowing costs, and compresses valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, positive 60-day SPX returns occur in more than 45% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) concurrent with decelerating nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals unsustainable consumption financed by drawing down savings or debt rather than income growth, which precedes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A joint model of RSAFS and PAYEMS fails to achieve a higher Rank IC than the best single-series model using either RSAFS or PAYEMS alone over any 3-year rolling window, indicating the signal is decomposable.

mechanism_narrative: During elevated unemployment regimes, a month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) reflects discouraged workers re-entering the labor force rather than net job losses, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with unemployment above 5 percent
failure_condition: Within months where UNRATE exceeds 5%, instances of rising UNRATE coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,200 thousand units indicates deteriorating residential investment and forward-looking recession risk, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1200, positive 60-day SPX returns occur in more than 45% of months, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) signals stronger nominal demand and pricing power for corporations, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in CPIAUCSL coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in fewer than 55% of weeks over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A tight labor market indicated by a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with decelerating nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) squeezes corporate profit margins through wage pressure without offsetting revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from UNRATE and PAYEMS does not outperform a linear combination of the two independent single-series signals over a 3-year rolling window, as measured by Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output and aggregate supply, which supports higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods of positive 3-month INDPRO growth coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns in fewer than 55% of quarters over any 3-year rolling window.
